mentions.note: Massive spending commitments and cash burn make a near-term IPO both less likely and riskier if it happens; Wall Street may balk once financials are disclosed.

$1.4T compute commitment vs. $20B annual revenue; must raise $100B+ at $800B+ valuation; competition from Gemini (Google) and Claude (Anthropic); Elon Musk lawsuit seeks $134B; biggest risk to AI data center complex in 2026.
Valued at $730B in recent private fundraising; massive private valuation; public reception unknown ahead of expected IPO in 2026.
Private company; Cramer noted ChatGPT and emergence of AI agents as huge opportunity driver for Nvidia infrastructure; not yet public.

Historic $120B+ fundraising round at $840B valuation; fastest-growing company on Earth with 900M weekly active users, 50M+ subscribers; path to IPO late 2026/early 2027; profitable enterprise growth and consumer subscriptions; force-multiplier AI narrative proven with customer wins (BBVA, Lowe's, Walmart).
Just raised $120B privately; no urgency to go public; building IPO outcome for future but no imminent timeline.
Valuation $852B; mega-IPO expected to follow SpaceX; risk of excess supply crowding out S&P 500.
AI king of creches; part of hardware/AI winning camp; positioned for pantheon of greatness alongside Anthropic as hardware/AI duo dominates software.
ChatGPT future usage momentum turning negative vs. Claude's dominance; if publicly traded would warrant 'head for the hills' despite still +10% momentum.
Fourth-layer AI model provider (ChatGPT); key player in AI revolution; pre-IPO.
Litigation with Musk ongoing (jury selection started); potential $1T+ valuation IPO; could compete with Anthropic timing; major capital drain risk if launches.
Wall Street Journal article raised concerns but likely overblown; CFO Sarah Fryer is trustworthy. Not a shorting opportunity but caution warranted on spend-without-revenue risk.
$852B valuation after $122B fundraising round (largest private raise in history). IPO capital raise combined with SpaceX/Anthropic creates unprecedented liquidity drain risk.
Major AI customer demanding massive data center infrastructure; driving AWS and hyperscaler capital spend.
Model builder spending massive amounts on AI infrastructure; part of AI revolution layer 4 (pre-IPO private; no price data).
Soon-to-be-public AI leader alongside Anthropic disrupting traditional expensive enterprise software; #1 on CNBC Disruptor 50.

Private company losing tons of money; IPO timing uncertain; could be real negative surprise if comes last in capital raise queue; not hearing signals it's ready for public markets; funding profile concerning.
More speculative than Anthropic without comparable revenue traction; private market valuations unsustainably high.
User intent metrics decent (top 10% of brands) but Cramer expresses structural valuation concerns; more worried than enthusiastic.
Not doing well; has some good people and products but not good balance sheet. Will probably be last in IPO shoot depending on deal terms; have to see where deal comes before jumping in.
Pre-IPO/private; good people and products but not good balance sheet; likely last in IPO queue; deal timing uncertain.
Powerful new AI finance competitor; always lurking threat to Mag 7; profitability not yet proven.
Delaying IPO to 2026 per NY Times citing AI stock declines and large losses; Cramer speculates potential Microsoft merger if IPO delayed beyond 2026; wants more time to digest IPO supply.
Massive private fundraising competes for the same capital pool; Cramer wants it public given its cash requirements.
Apple trade-secret suit plus the NYT copyright case and dozens of IP claims form a serious, non-dismissable overhang that clouds IPO prospects.
